name: PlanetScale MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official PlanetScale MCP server (integrated in the PlanetScale CLI) enabling AI agents to interact with PlanetScale databases — querying data, managing database branches, running schema migrations, and monitoring database status.
use_cases: Querying PlanetScale databases from agent workflows
Managing database branches for schema change workflows
Running and monitoring schema migrations via deploy requests
Checking database performance insights and slow query analysis
Automated schema drift detection between branches
not_for: High-frequency real-time transactions (PlanetScale is not a Redis replacement)
Teams not using PlanetScale's serverless MySQL
best_when: An agent needs to query or manage a PlanetScale MySQL database, particularly leveraging PlanetScale's branching and schema change workflow.
avoid_when: You're using PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or other non-MySQL databases.

auth.notes: Service tokens with fine-grained permissions (read vs write vs admin per database). OAuth for user-level auth. Service tokens recommended for agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Hobby: free with 5GB storage, 1 billion row reads/month
pricing.notes: Generous free tier. Row read pricing model — agents making many queries should monitor usage.

performance.rate_limits: Row reads billed; no explicit req/s limit documented for API

known_agent_gotchas: PlanetScale requires explicit branch selection — agents must specify the correct branch
Schema changes must go through deploy requests — DDL statements blocked on production branches
Row read billing means expensive agent queries (full table scans) accrue real costs
PlanetScale's sharding via Vitess means some MySQL queries not supported (foreign keys)
Database branches are ephemeral — agents should not hardcode branch names
agent_readiness.security_notes: Service tokens with per-database scopes provide good granularity. HTTPS enforced. No foreign key constraints by default (Vitess limitation). SOC 2 Type II.

security_score_components.security_notes: Service tokens with fine-grained per-database permissions. HTTPS enforced. SOC 2 Type II certified. Good overall security posture.
